member.remarks: Currently, the implementation only supports the echo message (better known as 'ping').

member.name: M:AbcLib.Net.Icmp.Ping
member.summary: Initiates an ICMP ping with a timeout of 1000 milliseconds.
member.exception: There was an error while communicating with the remote server.
member.returns: A TimeSpan object that holds the time it takes for a packet to travel to the remote server and back. A value of TimeSpan.MaxValue indicates a timeout.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Net.Icmp.Ping(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initiates an ICMP ping.
member.param: Specifies the timeout in milliseconds. If this value is set to Timeout.Infinite, the method will never time out.
member.returns: A TimeSpan object that holds the time it takes for a packet to travel to the remote server and back. A value of TimeSpan.MaxValue indicates a timeout.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Gui.Progress
member.summary: This class implement a generic fucntionality for showing a progress dialog box (please progress type) while the main process continues to process some time consuming tasks. The advantage of this class is that, the window is shown in a different thread, making the window user responsive and if the user presses a cancel button a boolen property gets marked that the user cancelled the window. In this case it's the calling programs job to query the Property time to time.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased
member.summary: Abstract base class for all XML-based Profile classes.
member.remarks: This class provides common methods and properties for the XML-based Profile classes (, ).

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile
member.summary: Abstract base class for all Profile classes in this namespace.
member.remarks: This class contains fields and methods which are common for all the derived Profile classes. It fully implements most of the methods and properties of its base interfaces so that derived classes don't have to.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile
member.summary: Interface implemented by all profile classes in this namespace. It represents a normal profile.
member.remarks: This interface takes the members of IReadOnlyProfile (its base interface) and adds to it the rest of the members, which allow modifications to the profile. Altogether, this represents a complete profile object.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile
member.summary: Base interface for all profile classes in this namespace. It represents a read-only profile.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
member.returns: The return value should be the entry's value, or null if the entry does not exist.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetValue(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section, or a default value if the entry does not exist.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
The value to return if the entry (or section) does not exist.
member.returns: The return value should be the entry's value converted to a string, or the given default value if the entry does not exist.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section, or a default value if the entry does not exist.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
The value to return if the entry (or section) does not exist.
member.returns: The return value should be the entry's value converted to an integer. If the value cannot be converted, the return value should be 0. If the entry does not exist, the given default value should be returned.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Double)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section, or a default value if the entry does not exist.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
The value to return if the entry (or section) does not exist.
member.returns: The return value should be the entry's value converted to a double. If the value cannot be converted, the return value should be 0. If the entry does not exist, the given default value should be returned.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section, or a default value if the entry does not exist.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
The value to return if the entry (or section) does not exist.
member.returns: The return value should be the entry's value converted to a bool. If the value cannot be converted, the return value should be false. If the entry does not exist, the given default value should be returned.
member.remarks: Note: Boolean values are stored as "True" or "False".

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.HasEntry(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Determines if an entry exists inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry to be checked for existence.
member.returns: If the entry exists inside the section, the return value should be true; otherwise false.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.HasSection(System.String)
member.summary: Determines if a section exists.
member.param: The name of the section to be checked for existence.
member.returns: If the section exists, the return value should be true; otherwise false.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetEntryNames(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the names of all the entries inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section holding the entries.
member.returns: If the section exists, the return value should be an array with the names of its entries; otherwise it should be null.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetSectionNames
member.summary: Retrieves the names of all the sections.
member.returns: The return value should be an array with the names of all the sections.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.GetDataSet
member.summary: Retrieves a DataSet object containing every section, entry, and value in the profile.
member.returns: If the profile exists, the return value should be a DataSet object representing the profile; otherwise it's null.
member.remarks:
The returned DataSet should be named using the property. It should contain one table for each section, and each entry should be represented by a column inside the table. Each table should contain only one row where the values will be stored corresponding to each column (entry).
This method serves as a convenient way to extract the profile's data to this generic medium known as the DataSet. This allows it to be moved to many different places, including a different type of profile object (eg., INI to XML conversion).

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.IReadOnlyProfile.Name
member.summary: Gets the name associated with the profile.
member.remarks: This should be the name of the file where the data is stored, or something equivalent.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.SetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the value for an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry where the value will be set.
The value to set. If it's null, the entry should be removed.
member.remarks: This method should check the property and throw an InvalidOperationException if it's true. It should also raise the and events before and after the value is set.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.RemoveEntry(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Removes an entry from a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry to remove.
member.remarks: This method should check the property and throw an InvalidOperationException if it's true. It should also raise the and events before and after the entry is removed.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.RemoveSection(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a section.
member.param: The name of the section to remove.
member.remarks: This method should check the property and throw an InvalidOperationException if it's true. It should also raise the and events before and after the section is removed.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.SetDataSet(System.Data.DataSet)
member.summary: Writes the data of every table from a DataSet into this profile.
member.param: The DataSet object containing the data to be set.
member.remarks:
Each table in the DataSet should be used to represent a section of the profile. Each column of each table should represent an entry. And for each column, the corresponding value of the first row is the value that should be passed to .
This method serves as a convenient way to take any data inside a generic DataSet and write it to any of the available profiles.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.CloneReadOnly
member.summary: Creates a copy of itself and makes it read-only.
member.returns: The return value should be a copy of itself as an IReadOnlyProfile object.
member.remarks: This method is meant as a convenient way to pass a read-only copy of the profile to methods that are not allowed to modify it.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name associated with the profile.
member.remarks: This should be the name of the file where the data is stored, or something equivalent. When setting this property, the property should be checked and if true, an InvalidOperationException should be raised. The and events should be raised before and after this property is changed.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.DefaultName
member.summary: Gets the name associated with the profile by default.
member.remarks: This is used to set the default Name of the profile and it is typically based on the name of the executable plus some extension.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.ReadOnly
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the profile is read-only or not.
member.remarks: A read-only profile should not allow any operations that alter sections, entries, or values, such as or . Once a profile has been marked read-only, it should be allowed to go back; attempting to do so should cause an InvalidOperationException to be raised. The and events should be raised before and after this property is changed.

member.name: E:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.Changing
member.summary: Event that should be raised just before the profile is to be changed to allow the change to be canceled.

member.name: E:AbcLib.Configuration.IProfile.Changed
member.summary: Event that should be raised right after the profile has been changed.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.GetDataSet
member.summary: Retrieves a DataSet object containing every section, entry, and value in the profile.
member.returns: If the profile exists, the return value is a DataSet object representing the profile; otherwise it's null.
member.exception: is null or empty.
member.remarks:
The returned DataSet will be named using the property. It will contain one table for each section, and each entry will be represented by a column inside the table. Each table will contain only one row where the values will stored corresponding to each column (entry).
This method serves as a convenient way to extract the profile's data to this generic medium known as the DataSet. This allows it to be moved to many different places, including a different type of profile object (eg., INI to XML conversion).
This method calls GetSectionNames, GetEntryNames, and GetValue of the derived class, so check the documentation to see what other exceptions may be raised.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.SetDataSet(System.Data.DataSet)
member.summary: Writes the data of every table from a DataSet into this profile.
member.param: The DataSet object containing the data to be set.
member.exception: is true or is null or empty.
ds is null.
member.remarks:
Each table in the DataSet represents a section of the profile. Each column of each table represents an entry. And for each column, the corresponding value of the first row is the value to be passed to . Note that only the first row is imported; additional rows are ignored.
This method serves as a convenient way to take any data inside a generic DataSet and write it to any of the available profiles.
This method calls SetValue of the derived class, so check its documentation to see what other exceptions may be raised.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.VerifyAndAdjustSection(System.String@)
member.summary: Verifies the given section name is not null and trims it.
member.param: The section name to verify and adjust.
member.exception: section is null.
member.remarks: This method may be used by derived classes to make sure that a valid section name has been passed, and to make any necessary adjustments to it before passing it to the corresponding APIs.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.VerifyAndAdjustEntry(System.String@)
member.summary: Verifies the given entry name is not null and trims it.
member.param: The entry name to verify and adjust.
member.remarks: This method may be used by derived classes to make sure that a valid entry name has been passed, and to make any necessary adjustments to it before passing it to the corresponding APIs.
member.exception: entry is null.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.VerifyName
member.summary: Verifies the Name property is not empty or null.
member.remarks: This method may be used by derived classes to make sure that the APIs are working with a valid Name (file name)
member.exception: name is empty or null.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.VerifyNotReadOnly
member.summary: Verifies the ReadOnly property is not true.
member.remarks: This method may be used by derived classes as a convenient way to validate that modifications to the profile can be made.
member.exception: ReadOnly is true.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.RaiseChangeEvent(System.Boolean,AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangeType,System.String,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Raises either the Changing or Changed event.
member.param: If true, the Changing event is raised otherwise it's Changed.
The type of change being made.
The name of the section that was involved in the change or null if not applicable.
The name of the entry that was involved in the change or null if not applicable. If changeType is equal to Other, entry is the name of the property involved in the change.
The value that was changed or null if not applicable.
member.returns: The return value is based on the event raised. If the Changing event was raised, the return value is the opposite of ProfileChangingArgs.Cancel; otherwise it's true.
member.remarks: This method may be used by derived classes as a convenient alternative to calling OnChanging and OnChanged. For example, a typical call to OnChanging would require four lines of code, which this method reduces to two.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.OnChanging(AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangingArgs)
member.summary: Raises the Changing event.
member.param: The arguments object associated with the Changing event.
member.remarks: This method should be invoked prior to making a change to the profile so that the Changing event is raised, giving a chance to the handlers to prevent the change from happening (by setting e.Cancel to true). This method calls each individual handler associated with the Changing event and checks the resulting e.Cancel flag. If it's true, it stops and does not call of any remaining handlers since the change needs to be prevented anyway.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.OnChanged(AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangedArgs)
member.summary: Raises the Changed event.
member.param: The arguments object associated with the Changed event.
member.remarks: This method should be invoked after a change to the profile has been made so that the Changed event is raised, giving a chance to the handlers to be notified of the change.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.Test(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Runs a test to verify this object is working as expected.
member.param: If true, the modifications made to the profile are cleaned up as the final part of the test. If false, the modifications are not removed thus allowing them to be examined.
member.remarks: This method tests most of the funcionality of a profile object to ensure accuracy and consistency. All profile classes should behave identically when calling this method. If the test fails, an Exception is raised detailing the problem.
member.exception: The test failed.

member.name: E:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.Changing
member.summary: Event used to notify that the profile is about to be changed.

member.name: E:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.Changed
member.summary: Event used to notify that the profile has been changed.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name associated with the profile.
member.exception: Setting this property to null.
Setting this property if ReadOnly is true.
member.remarks: This is usually the name of the file where the data is stored. The event is raised before changing this property. If its property is set to true, this property returns immediately without being changed. After the property is changed, the event is raised.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.ReadOnly
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the profile is read-only or not.
member.exception: Setting this property if it's already true.
member.remarks: A read-only profile does not allow any operations that alter sections, entries, or values, such as or . Once a profile has been marked read-only, it may no longer go back; attempting to do so causes an InvalidOperationException to be raised. The event is raised before changing this property. If its property is set to true, this property returns immediately without being changed. After the property is changed, the event is raised.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.DefaultName
member.summary: Gets the name associated with the profile by default.
member.remarks: This property needs to be implemented by derived classes. See IProfile.DefaultName for additional remarks.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Profile.DefaultNameWithoutExtension
member.summary: Gets the name of the file to be used as the default, without the profile-specific extension.
member.remarks:
This property is used by file-based Profile implementations when composing the DefaultName. These implementations take the value returned by this property and add their own specific extension (.ini, .xml, .config, etc.).
For Windows applications, this property returns the full path of the executable. For Web applications, this returns the full path of the web.config file without the .config extension.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the XmlBased class by setting the to .

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the XmlBased class by setting the to the given file name.
member.param: The name of the file to initialize the property with.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.#ctor(AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the XmlBased class based on another XmlBased object.
member.param: The XmlBased profile object whose properties and events are used to initialize the object being constructed.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.GetXmlDocument
member.summary: Retrieves an XmlDocument object based on the of the file.
member.returns: If is not enabled, the return value is the XmlDocument object loaded with the file, or null if the file does not exist. If is enabled, the return value is an XmlDocument object, which will be loaded with the file if it already exists.
member.exception: is null or empty.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.Save(System.Xml.XmlDocument)
member.summary: Saves any changes pending on an XmlDocument object, unless is enabled.
member.exception: The resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks: If is enabled, this method sets the property to true and the changes are not saved until the buffer is flushed (or closed). If the Buffer is not active the contents of the XmlDocument object are saved to the file.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.Buffer(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Activates buffering on this XML-based profile object, if not already active.
member.param: If true, the file is locked when the buffer is activated so that no other processes can write to it. If false, other processes can continue writing to it and the actual contents of the file can get out of synch with the contents of the buffer.
member.returns: The return value is an object that may be used to control the buffer used to read/write values from this XmlBased profile.
member.exception: Attempting to lock the file and the name is null or empty.
Attempting to lock the file without the required permission.
Attempting to lock the file and ReadWrite access is not permitted by the operating system.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.Buffer
member.summary: Activates locked buffering on this XML-based profile object, if not already active.
member.returns: The return value is an object that may be used to control the buffer used to read/write values from this XmlBased profile.
member.exception: Attempting to lock the file and the name is null or empty.
Attempting to lock the file without the required permission.
Attempting to lock the file and ReadWrite access is not permitted by the operating system.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.Buffering
member.summary: Gets whether buffering is active or not.
member.remarks:
Buffering is the caching of an object so that subsequent reads or writes are all done through it. This dramatically increases the performance of those operations, but it requires that the buffer is flushed (or closed) to commit any changes done to the underlying file.
This property may be used to determine if the buffer is active without actually activating it.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased.Encoding
member.summary: Gets or sets the encoding, to be used if the file is created.
member.exception: Setting this property if is true.
member.remarks: By default this property is set to Encoding.UTF8, but it is only used when the file is not found and needs to be created to write the value. If the file exists, the existing encoding is used and this value is ignored. The event is raised before changing this property. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without changing this property. After the property has been changed, the event is raised.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer
member.summary: Buffer class for all Profile classes.
member.remarks:
This class provides buffering functionality for the classes. Buffering refers to the caching of an object so that subsequent reads or writes are all done through it. This dramatically increases the performance of those operations, but it requires that the buffer is flushed (or closed) to commit any changes done to the underlying file.
Since an XmlBased object can only have one buffer attached to it at a time, this class may not be instanciated directly. Instead, use the "XmlBased.Buffer" method of the profile object.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.#ctor(AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBased,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the XmlBuffer class and optionally locks the file.
member.param: The XmlBased object to associate with the buffer and to assign this object to.
If true and the file exists, the file is locked to prevent other processes from writing to it until the buffer is closed.
member.exception: Attempting to lock the file and the name is null or empty.
Attempting to lock the file without the required permission.
Attempting to lock the file and ReadWrite access is not permitted by the operating system.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.Load(System.Xml.XmlTextWriter)
member.summary: Loads the XmlDocument object with the contents of an XmlTextWriter object.
member.param: The XmlTextWriter object to load the XmlDocument with.
member.remarks: This method is used to load the buffer with new data.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.Flush
member.summary: Writes the contents of the XmlDocument object to the file associated with this buffer's profile.
member.remarks: This method may be used to explictly commit any changes made to the profile from the time the buffer was last flushed or created. It writes the contents of the XmlDocument object to the profile's file. When the buffer is being closed (with or ) this method is called if is true. After the buffer is closed, this method may not be called.
member.exception: This object is closed.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.Reset
member.summary: Resets the buffer by discarding its XmlDocument object.
member.remarks: This method may be used to rollback any changes made to the profile from the time the buffer was last flushed or created. After the buffer is closed, this method may not be called.
member.exception: This object is closed.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.Close
member.summary: Closes the buffer by flushing the contents of its XmlDocument object (if necessary) and dettaching itself from its profile.
member.remarks: This method may be used to explictly deactivate the profile buffer. This means that the buffer is flushed (if is true) and it gets dettached from the profile. The method automatically calls this method.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes of this object's resources by closing the buffer.
member.remarks: This method calls , which flushes the buffer and dettaches it from the profile.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.XmlDocument
member.summary: Gets the XmlDocument object associated with this buffer, based on the profile's Name.
member.exception: is null or empty.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.IsEmpty
member.summary: Gets whether the buffer's XmlDocument object is empty.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.NeedsFlushing
member.summary: Gets whether changes have been made to the XmlDocument object that require the buffer to be flushed so that the file gets updated.
member.remarks: This property returns true when the XmlDocument object has been changed and the (or ) method needs to be called to update the file.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.XmlBuffer.Locked
member.summary: Gets whether the file associated with the buffer's profile is locked.
member.remarks: This property returns true when this object has been created with the lockFile parameter set to true, provided the file exists. When locked, other processes will not be allowed to write to the profile's file until the buffer is closed.

member.summary: Retrieves information about the processor.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetVendorID(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Retrieves a 12-byte string that contains the Vendor ID (ie. 'GenuineIntel', 'AuthenticAMD', ...)
member.param: The string buffer that will receive the vendor ID.
member.remarks: The buffer must be at least 13 characters long [12 + null char].

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetProcessorFeatures
member.summary: Returns an integer that contains the processor features.
member.remarks: The following list is a description of the bits and their meaning: bit 31 reserved bit 30 (IA-64) bit 29 (TM) Thermal monitor supported bit 28 (HTT) Hyper-Threading Technology bit 27 (SS) selfsnoop bit 26 (SSE2) Streaming SIMD extensions 2 supported bit 25 (SSE) Streaming SIMD extensions supported bit 24 (FXSR) Fast floating point save and restore bit 23 (MMX) MMX supported bit 22 (ACPI) bit 21 (DTES) Debug Trace and EMON Store MSRs bit 20 reserved bit 19 (CLFL) CLFLUSH instruction supported bit 18 (PSN) Processor serial number present and enabled bit 17 (PSE36) 36-bit page size extension bit 16 (PAT) Page attribute table bit 15 (CMOV) Conditional move instruction supported bit 14 (MCA) Machine check architecture bit 13 (PGE) Page global enabled bit 12 (MTRR) Memory type range registers bit 11 (SEP) bit 10 reserved bit 9 (APIC) On-chip APIC hardware supported bit 8 (CX8) SMPXCHG instruction supported bit 7 (MCE) Machine check extension bit 6 (PAE) Physical address extension bit 5 (MSR) Model specific registers bit 4 (TSC) Time stamp counter bit 3 (PSE) Page size extension bit 2 (DE) Debugging extension bit 1 (VME) Virtual Mode extension bit 0 (FPU) Floating point unit on-chip
member.returns: An integer value.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Has3DNowAsm
member.summary: Checks for 3DNow! support.
member.returns: A non-zero value if the processor supports 3DNow, zero otherwise.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Has3DNowExtAsm
member.summary: Checks for 3DNow! Extensions support.
member.returns: A non-zero value if the processor supports 3DNow Extensions, zero otherwise.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.HasMmx
member.summary: Checks for MMX support.
member.returns: A non-zero value if the processor supports MMX, zero otherwise.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.HasSerialNumber
member.summary: Checks for the presence of a processor serial number.
member.returns: A non-zero value if the processor has a serial number, zero otherwise.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetProcessorFamily
member.summary: Returns the processor family number.
member.returns: An integer value.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetProcessorModel
member.summary: Returns the processor model number.
member.returns: An integer value.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetProcessorStepping
member.summary: Returns the processor stepping number.
member.returns: An integer value.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetProcessorSerial(System.Int64@)
member.summary: Retrieves the processor serial number (if it has one).
member.param: The variable that receives the serial number.
member.returns: Returns zero if an error occurred, a non-zero value otherwise.
member.remarks: Processor manufacturers cannot guarantee that a processor serial number is unique.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.GetProcessorName(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: Retrieves the name of the processor.
member.param: The string buffer that receives the processor name.
member.remarks: The size of the buffer must be at least 49 characters (48 + NULL char). The returned buffer can be empty on processors that do not support the processor name function (Intel Pentium III and older, for instance).

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new Processor instance.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Has3DNow
member.summary: Checks whether the processor supports 3D Now!.
member.value: true if the processor supports 3D Now!, false otherwise.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Has3DNowExt
member.summary: Checks whether the processor supports 3D Now! extensions.
member.value: true if the processor supports 3D Now! extensions, false otherwise.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Name
member.summary: Gets the name of the processor.
member.value: A string that contains the name of the processor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Vendor
member.summary: Gets the name of the vendor of the processor.
member.value: A string that contains the name of the vendor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Version
member.summary: Gets a instance that contains the processor build number.
member.value: An instance of the Version class.
member.remarks: Major corresponds to Family, Minor corresponds to Model and Build corresponds to Stepping.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Family
member.summary: Gets the family of the processor.
member.value: An integer that contains the family number of the processor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Model
member.summary: Gets the model of the processor.
member.value: An integer that contains the model number of the processor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Stepping
member.summary: Gets the stepping of the processor.
member.value: An integer that contains the stepping number of the processor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Features
member.summary: Gets the processor features.
member.value: A combination of the ProcessorFeatures fields.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.CpuInfo.Serial
member.summary: Gets the serial number of the processor.
member.value: A long that contains the serial number of the processor.
member.exception: The processor does not have a serial number.

member.summary: Searches the chart to find the index of the pie slice which contains point given. Search order goes in the direction opposite to drawing order.
member.param: PointF point for which pie slice is searched for.
member.returns: Index of the corresponding pie slice, or -1 if none is found.

member.param: Array of PieSlice objects to examine.
member.returns: Index of the foremost pie slice.

member.summary: Readjusts each slice for new bounding rectangle.
member.param: RectangleF representing new boundary.

members.member:
Initializes pies.
Creates a list of pies, starting with the pie that is crossing the 270 degrees boundary, i.e. "backmost" pie that always has to be drawn first to ensure correct surface overlapping.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Gui.ProgressEx
member.summary: This class implement a generic fucntionality for showing a progress dialog box (please progress type) while the main process continues to process some time consuming tasks. The advantage of this class is that, the window is shown in a different thread, making the window user responsive and if the user presses a cancel button a boolen property gets marked that the user cancelled the window. In this case it's the calling programs job to query the Property time to time.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml
member.summary: Profile class that utilizes an XML file to retrieve and save its data.
member.remarks:
This class works with XML files, which are text files that store their data using XML. Since the format of XML is very flexible, I had to decide how to best organize the data using the section/entry paradigm. After considering a couple of possibilities, I decided that the format below would be preferrable, since it allows section and entry names to contain spaces. It also looks cleaner and more consistent than if I had used the section and entry names themselves to name the elements.
Here's an illustration of the format:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <profile> <section name="A Section"> <entry name="An Entry">Some Value</entry> <entry name="Another Entry">Another Value</entry> </section> <section name="Another Section"> <entry name="This is cool">True</entry> </section> </profile>

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Xml class by setting the to .

member.param: The name of the XML file to initialize the property with.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml.SetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the value for an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry where the value will be set.
The value to set. If it's null, the entry is removed.
member.exception: is null or empty, is true, or the resulting XML document is invalid.
Either section or entry is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file or the resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks:
If the XML file does not exist, it is created, unless is enabled. The event is raised before setting the value. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without setting the value. After the value has been set, the event is raised.
Note: If is enabled, the value is not actually written to the XML file until the buffer is flushed (or closed).

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml.GetValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
member.returns: The return value is the entry's value, or null if the entry does not exist.
member.exception: is null or empty.
Either section or entry is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml.RemoveEntry(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Removes an entry from a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry to remove.
member.exception: is null or empty or is true.
Either section or entry is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file or the resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks:
The event is raised before removing the entry. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without removing the entry. After the entry has been removed, the event is raised.
Note: If is enabled, the entry is not removed from the XML file until the buffer is flushed (or closed).

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml.RemoveSection(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a section.
member.param: The name of the section to remove.
member.exception: is null or empty or is true.
section is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file or the resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks:
The event is raised before removing the section. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without removing the section. After the section has been removed, the event is raised.
Note: If is enabled, the section is not removed from the XML file until the buffer is flushed (or closed).

member.summary: Retrieves the names of all the entries inside a section.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml.DefaultName
member.summary: Gets the default name for the XML file.
member.remarks: For Windows apps, this property returns the name of the executable plus .xml ("program.exe.xml"). For Web apps, this property returns the full path of web.xml based on the root folder. This property is used to set the property inside the default constructor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Xml.RootName
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the root element, to be used if the file is created.
member.exception: Setting this property if is true.
Setting this property to null.
member.remarks: By default this property is set to "profile", but it is only used when the file is not found and needs to be created to write the value. If the file exists, the name of the root element inside the file is ignored. The event is raised before changing this property. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without changing this property. After the property has been changed, the event is raised.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions
member.summary: Specifies the type of restart options that an application can use.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions.LogOff
member.summary: Shuts down all processes running in the security context of the process that called the ExitWindowsEx function. Then it logs the user off.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions.PowerOff
member.summary: Shuts down the system and turns off the power. The system must support the power-off feature.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions.Reboot
member.summary: Shuts down the system and then restarts the system.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions.ShutDown
member.summary: Shuts down the system to a point at which it is safe to turn off the power. All file buffers have been flushed to disk, and all running processes have stopped. If the system supports the power-off feature, the power is also turned off.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions.Suspend
member.summary: Suspends the system.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions.Hibernate
member.summary: Hibernates the system.

member.summary: The low order part of the 64 bit value.

member.summary: Specifies an LUID value.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.LUID_AND_ATTRIBUTES.Attributes

member.summary: Specifies the number of entries in the Privileges array.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.TOKEN_PRIVILEGES.Privileges

member.summary: Required to enable or disable the privileges in an access token.

member.summary: Required to query an access token.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.SE_PRIVILEGE_ENABLED
member.summary: The privilege is enabled.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.FORMAT_MESSAGE_FROM_SYSTEM
member.summary: Specifies that the function should search the system message-table resource(s) for the requested message.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.EWX_FORCE
member.summary: Forces processes to terminate. When this flag is set, the system does not send the WM_QUERYENDSESSION and WM_ENDSESSION messages. This can cause the applications to lose data. Therefore, you should only use this flag in an emergency.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.LoadLibrary(System.String)
member.summary: The LoadLibrary function maps the specified executable module into the address space of the calling process.
member.param: Pointer to a null-terminated string that names the executable module (either a .dll or .exe file). The name specified is the file name of the module and is not related to the name stored in the library module itself, as specified by the LIBRARY keyword in the module-definition (.def) file.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is a handle to the module.
If the function fails, the return value is NULL. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.FreeLibrary(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: The FreeLibrary function decrements the reference count of the loaded dynamic-link library (DLL). When the reference count reaches zero, the module is unmapped from the address space of the calling process and the handle is no longer valid.
member.param: Handle to the loaded DLL module. The LoadLibrary or GetModuleHandle function returns this handle.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero.
If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.GetProcAddress(System.IntPtr,System.String)
member.summary: The GetProcAddress function retrieves the address of an exported function or variable from the specified dynamic-link library (DLL).
member.param: Handle to the DLL module that contains the function or variable. The LoadLibrary or GetModuleHandle function returns this handle.

Pointer to a null-terminated string containing the function or variable name, or the function's ordinal value. If this parameter is an ordinal value, it must be in the low-order word; the high-order word must be zero.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is the address of the exported function or variable.
If the function fails, the return value is NULL. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.SetSuspendState(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: The SetSuspendState function suspends the system by shutting power down. Depending on the Hibernate parameter, the system either enters a suspend (sleep) state or hibernation (S4). If the ForceFlag parameter is TRUE, the system suspends operation immediately; if it is FALSE, the system requests permission from all applications and device drivers before doing so.
member.param: Specifies the state of the system. If TRUE, the system hibernates. If FALSE, the system is suspended.

Forced suspension. If TRUE, the function broadcasts a PBT_APMSUSPEND event to each application and driver, then immediately suspends operation. If FALSE, the function broadcasts a PBT_APMQUERYSUSPEND event to each application to request permission to suspend operation.
If TRUE, the system disables all wake events. If FALSE, any system wake events remain enabled.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero.
If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.OpenProcessToken(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: The OpenProcessToken function opens the access token associated with a process.
member.param: Handle to the process whose access token is opened.

Specifies an access mask that specifies the requested types of access to the access token. These requested access types are compared with the token's discretionary access-control list (DACL) to determine which accesses are granted or denied.
Pointer to a handle identifying the newly-opened access token when the function returns.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero.
If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.LookupPrivilegeValue(System.String,System.String,AbcLib.Sys.LUID@)
member.summary: The LookupPrivilegeValue function retrieves the locally unique identifier (LUID) used on a specified system to locally represent the specified privilege name.
member.param: Pointer to a null-terminated string specifying the name of the system on which the privilege name is looked up. If a null string is specified, the function attempts to find the privilege name on the local system.

Pointer to a null-terminated string that specifies the name of the privilege, as defined in the Winnt.h header file. For example, this parameter could specify the constant SE_SECURITY_NAME, or its corresponding string, "SeSecurityPrivilege".
Pointer to a variable that receives the locally unique identifier by which the privilege is known on the system, specified by the lpSystemName parameter.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero.
If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.AdjustTokenPrivileges(System.IntPtr,System.Int32,AbcLib.Sys.TOKEN_PRIVILEGES@,System.Int32,AbcLib.Sys.TOKEN_PRIVILEGES@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: The AdjustTokenPrivileges function enables or disables privileges in the specified access token. Enabling or disabling privileges in an access token requires TOKEN_ADJUST_PRIVILEGES access.
member.param: Handle to the access token that contains the privileges to be modified. The handle must have TOKEN_ADJUST_PRIVILEGES access to the token. If the PreviousState parameter is not NULL, the handle must also have TOKEN_QUERY access.

Specifies whether the function disables all of the token's privileges. If this value is TRUE, the function disables all privileges and ignores the NewState parameter. If it is FALSE, the function modifies privileges based on the information pointed to by the NewState parameter.

Pointer to a TOKEN_PRIVILEGES structure that specifies an array of privileges and their attributes. If the DisableAllPrivileges parameter is FALSE, AdjustTokenPrivileges enables or disables these privileges for the token. If you set the SE_PRIVILEGE_ENABLED attribute for a privilege, the function enables that privilege; otherwise, it disables the privilege. If DisableAllPrivileges is TRUE, the function ignores this parameter.
Specifies the size, in bytes, of the buffer pointed to by the PreviousState parameter. This parameter can be zero if the PreviousState parameter is NULL.
Pointer to a buffer that the function fills with a TOKEN_PRIVILEGES structure that contains the previous state of any privileges that the function modifies. This parameter can be NULL.
Pointer to a variable that receives the required size, in bytes, of the buffer pointed to by the PreviousState parameter. This parameter can be NULL if PreviousState is NULL.
member.returns: If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero. To determine whether the function adjusted all of the specified privileges, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.ExitWindowsEx(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: The ExitWindowsEx function either logs off the current user, shuts down the system, or shuts down and restarts the system. It sends the WM_QUERYENDSESSION message to all applications to determine if they can be terminated.
member.param: Specifies the type of shutdown.
This parameter is ignored.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero.
If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.FormatMessage(System.Int32,System.IntPtr,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Text.StringBuilder,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: The FormatMessage function formats a message string. The function requires a message definition as input. The message definition can come from a buffer passed into the function. It can come from a message table resource in an already-loaded module. Or the caller can ask the function to search the system's message table resource(s) for the message definition. The function finds the message definition in a message table resource based on a message identifier and a language identifier. The function copies the formatted message text to an output buffer, processing any embedded insert sequences if requested.
member.param: Specifies aspects of the formatting process and how to interpret the lpSource parameter. The low-order byte of dwFlags specifies how the function handles line breaks in the output buffer. The low-order byte can also specify the maximum width of a formatted output line.
Specifies the location of the message definition. The type of this parameter depends upon the settings in the dwFlags parameter.
Specifies the message identifier for the requested message. This parameter is ignored if dwFlags includes FORMAT_MESSAGE_FROM_STRING.
Specifies the language identifier for the requested message. This parameter is ignored if dwFlags includes FORMAT_MESSAGE_FROM_STRING.

Pointer to a buffer for the formatted (and null-terminated) message. If dwFlags includes FORMAT_MESSAGE_ALLOCATE_BUFFER, the function allocates a buffer using the LocalAlloc function, and places the pointer to the buffer at the address specified in lpBuffer.

If the FORMAT_MESSAGE_ALLOCATE_BUFFER flag is not set, this parameter specifies the maximum number of TCHARs that can be stored in the output buffer. If FORMAT_MESSAGE_ALLOCATE_BUFFER is set, this parameter specifies the minimum number of TCHARs to allocate for an output buffer. For ANSI text, this is the number of bytes; for Unicode text, this is the number of characters.

Pointer to an array of values that are used as insert values in the formatted message. A %1 in the format string indicates the first value in the Arguments array; a %2 indicates the second argument; and so on.
member.returns:
If the function succeeds, the return value is the number of TCHARs stored in the output buffer, excluding the terminating null character.
If the function fails, the return value is zero. To get extended error information, call Marshal.GetLastWin32Error.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.ExitWindows(AbcLib.Sys.RestartOptions,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Exits windows (and tries to enable any required access rights, if necesarry).
member.param: One of the RestartOptions values that specifies how to exit windows.
True if the exit has to be forced, false otherwise.
member.exception: There was an error while requesting a required privilege.
The requested exit method is not supported on this platform.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.WindowsController.ExitWindows(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Exits windows (and tries to enable any required access rights, if necesarry).
member.param: One of the RestartOptions values that specifies how to exit windows.
True if the exit has to be forced, false otherwise.
member.remarks: This method cannot hibernate or suspend the system.
member.exception: There was an error while requesting a required privilege.

member.summary: Retrieves whether we don't have a valid GroupName and a given section is equal to "appSettings".
member.remarks: This method helps us determine whether we need to deal with the "configuration\configSections" element.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.VerifyAndAdjustSection(System.String@)
member.summary: Verifies the given section name is not null and trims it.
member.param: The section name to verify and adjust.
member.exception: section is null.
member.remarks: This method first calls Profile.VerifyAndAdjustSection and then replaces any spaces in the section with underscores. This is needed because XML element names may not contain spaces.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.SetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the value for an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry where the value will be set.
The value to set. If it's null, the entry is removed.
member.exception: is null or empty, is true, or the resulting XML document is invalid.
Either section or entry is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file or the resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks:
If the Config file does not exist, it is created. The event is raised before setting the value. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without setting the value. After the value has been set, the event is raised.
Note: If is enabled, the value is not actually written to the Config file until the buffer is flushed (or closed).

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.GetValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
member.returns: The return value is the entry's value, or null if the entry does not exist.
member.exception: is null or empty.
Either section or entry is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file.
'value' attribute is missing from the entry node.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.RemoveEntry(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Removes an entry from a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry to remove.
member.exception: is null or empty or is true.
Either section or entry is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file or the resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks:
The event is raised before removing the entry. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without removing the entry. After the entry has been removed, the event is raised.
Note: If is enabled, the entry is not removed from the Config file until the buffer is flushed (or closed).

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.RemoveSection(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a section.
member.param: The name of the section to remove.
member.exception: is null or empty or is true.
section is null.
Parse error in the XML being loaded from the file or the resulting XML document would not be well formed.
member.remarks:
The event is raised before removing the section. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without removing the section. After the section has been removed, the event is raised.
Note: If is enabled, the section is not removed from the Config file until the buffer is flushed (or closed).

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.DefaultName
member.summary: Gets the default name for the Config file.
member.remarks: For Windows apps, this property returns the name of the executable plus .config ("program.exe.config"). For Web apps, this property returns the full path of the web.config file. This property is used to set the property inside the default constructor.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.GroupName
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the element under which all sections should be located.
member.exception: Setting this property if is true.
The value being set contains an namespace prefix (eg, prefix:whatever).
member.remarks:
By default this property is set to "profile". This means that the sections come as descendants of "configuration\profile". However, this property may be set to null so that all sections can be placed directly under "configuration". This is useful for reading/writing the popular "appSettings" section, which may also be retrieved via the System.Configuration.ConfigurationSettings.AppSettings property.
The event is raised before changing this property. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without changing this property. After the property has been changed, the event is raised.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.HasGroupName
member.summary: Gets whether we have a valid GroupName.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.Config.GroupNameSlash
member.summary: Gets the name of the GroupName plus a slash or an empty string is HasGroupName is false.
member.remarks: This property helps us when retrieving sections.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.Ini
member.summary: Profile class that utilizes an INI-formatted file to retrieve and save its data.
member.remarks:
This class works with INI files, which use a simple two-dimensional text format of sections and entries to organize their data. Here's a sample of the format:
[A Section] An Entry=Some Value Another Entry=Another Value
[Another Section] This is cool=True
This class utilizes the GetPrivateProfileString" and "WritePrivateProfileString" Win32 APIs to read and write the INI file.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Win.Controls.NumericTextBox
member.summary: The numeric texbox takes numeric(decimal) values as input.
member.remarks: It has the following extra properties: NumericPrecision: Precision NumericScaleOnFocus: The scale to display when the textbox has got the focus. NumericScaleOnLostFocus: The scale to display when the textbox hasn't got the focus. NumericValue: The current numeric value displayed in the textbox (decimal) ZeroIsValid: Zero is a valid as input AllowNegative: Allow input of negative decimal numbers It has the following extra events: NumericValueChanged: The event fires when the numeric value changes by user input or programmaticly.(Like TextChanged) Use NumericValueChanged event instead of the TextChanged event!!!! The NumericValue property is also capable of databinding. The decimal number is displayed with grouping char.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch
member.summary: Represents a high-resolution stopwatch. It can measure time intervals up to a tenth of a millisecond.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.QueryPerformanceCounter(System.Int64@)
member.summary: The QueryPerformanceCounter function retrieves the current value of the high-resolution performance counter.
member.param: Pointer to a variable that receives the current performance-counter value, in counts.
member.returns: If the function succeeds, the return value is nonzero.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.QueryPerformanceFrequency(System.Int64@)
member.summary: The QueryPerformanceFrequency function retrieves the frequency of the high-resolution performance counter, if one exists. The frequency cannot change while the system is running.
member.param: Pointer to a variable that receives the current performance-counter frequency, in counts per second. If the installed hardware does not support a high-resolution performance counter, this parameter can be zero.
member.returns: If the installed hardware supports a high-resolution performance counter, the return value is nonzero.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the StopWatch class.
member.exception: The system does not have a high-resolution performance counter.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.Reset
member.summary: Resets the stopwatch. This method should be called when you start measuring.
member.exception: The system does not have a high-resolution performance counter.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.Peek
member.summary: Returns the time that has passed since the Reset() method was called.
member.remarks: The time is returned in tenths-of-a-millisecond. If the Peek method returns '10000', it means the interval took exactely one second.
member.returns: A long that contains the time that has passed since the Reset() method was called.
member.exception: The system does not have a high-resolution performance counter.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.GetValue
member.summary: Retrieves the current value of the high-resolution performance counter.
member.exception: The system does not have a high-resolution performance counter.
member.returns: A long that contains the current performance-counter value, in counts.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.GetFrequency
member.summary: Retrieves the frequency of the high-resolution performance counter, if one exists. The frequency cannot change while the system is running.
member.exception: The system does not have a high-resolution performance counter.
member.returns: A long that contains the current performance-counter frequency, in counts per second.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.m_StartTime
member.summary: Holds the value of the StartTime property.

member.name: F:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.m_Frequency
member.summary: Holds the value of the Frequency property.

member.value: A long that holds the start time.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Sys.StopWatch.Frequency
member.summary: Gets or sets the frequency of the high-resolution performance counter.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Win.Controls.ProgressTaskList
member.summary: Implements a task-list control, derived from Panel. It displays a vertical list of tasks, with an arrow beside the current task. When the task is complete, a green 'tick' icon is placed next to it to indicate that is is finished. To use this control, you must call Start() to begin, and NextTask() to advance to the next task.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangedArgs
member.summary: EventArgs class to be passed as the second parameter of a event handler.
member.remarks: This class provides all the information relevant to the change made to the Profile. It is also used as a convenient base class for the ProfileChangingArgs class which is passed as the second parameter of the event handler.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the ProfileChangedArgs class by initializing all of its properties.
member.param: The type of change made to the profile.
The name of the section involved in the change or null.
The name of the entry involved in the change, or if changeType is set to Other, the name of the method/property that was changed.
The new value for the entry or method/property, based on the value of changeType.

member.summary: Gets the type of change that raised the event.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangedArgs.Section
member.summary: Gets the name of the section involved in the change, or null if not applicable.

member.summary: Gets the name of the entry involved in the change, or null if not applicable.
member.remarks: If is set to Other, this property holds the name of the method/property that was changed.

member.name: P:AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangedArgs.Value
member.summary: Gets the new value for the entry or method/property, based on the value of .

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.ProfileChangingArgs
member.summary: EventArgs class to be passed as the second parameter of a event handler.
member.remarks: This class provides all the information relevant to the change about to be made to the Profile. Besides the properties of ProfileChangedArgs, it adds the Cancel property which allows the event handler to prevent the change from happening.

The new value for the entry or method/property, based on the value of changeType.

member.summary: Gets or sets whether the change about to the made should be canceled or not.
member.remarks: By default this property is set to false, meaning that the change is allowed.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Configuration.Registry
member.summary: Profile class that utilizes the Windows Registry to retrieve and save its data.
member.remarks:
By default class this class uses the HKEY_CURRENT_USER root key, and sets its default subkey based on the CompanyName and ProductName properties of the Application object. For the Demo application, the Company name is set to "AMS" and the product is "ProfileDemo". So the entire path looks like this:
HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\AMS\ProfileDemo
Each section is then created as a subkey of this location on the registry.

member.summary: Retrieves a copy of itself.
member.returns: The return value is a copy of itself as an object.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Registry.GetSubKey(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Retrieves a RegistryKey object for the given section.
member.param: The name of the section to retrieve the key for.
If true, the key is created if necessary; otherwise it is just opened.
If true the key is opened with write access; otherwise it is opened read-only.
member.returns: The return value is a RegistryKey object representing the section's subkey.
member.exception: The length of Name\section is longer than 255 characters.
The user does not have RegistryPermission.SetInclude(create, currentKey) or RegistryPermission.SetInclude(open, currentKey) access.
The registry key cannot be written to (for example, it was opened as an unwritable key)
member.remarks: This method returns a key for the equivalent path: + "\\" + + "\\" + section

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Registry.SetValue(System.String,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the value for an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry where the value will be set.
The value to set. If it's null, the entry is removed.
member.exception: is true or is null or empty.
Either section or entry is null, or the length of Name\section is longer than 255 characters.
The user does not have RegistryPermission.SetInclude(create, currentKey) or RegistryPermission.SetInclude(open, currentKey) access.
The registry key cannot be written to (for example, it was opened as an unwritable key)
member.remarks: If either the subkey, section, or entry does not exist, it is created. The event is raised before setting the value. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without setting the value. After the value has been set, the event is raised.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Registry.GetValue(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of an entry inside a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry with the value.
The name of the entry where the value is stored.
member.returns: The return value is the entry's value, or null if the entry does not exist.
member.exception: Either section or entry is null, or the length of Name\section is longer than 255 characters.
The user does not have RegistryPermission.SetInclude(delete, currentKey) access.
The registry key cannot be written to (for example, it was opened as an unwritable key)

member.name: M:AbcLib.Configuration.Registry.RemoveEntry(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Removes an entry from a section.
member.param: The name of the section that holds the entry.
The name of the entry to remove.
member.exception: is true.
Either section or entry is null, or the length of Name\section is longer than 255 characters.
The user does not have RegistryPermission.SetInclude(create, currentKey) or RegistryPermission.SetInclude(open, currentKey) access.
The registry key cannot be written to (for example, it was opened as an unwritable key)
member.remarks: The event is raised before removing the entry. If its property is set to true, this method returns immediately without removing the entry. After the entry has been removed, the event is raised.

member.summary: Removes a section.

section is null or the length of Name\section is longer than 255 characters.

member.name: T:AbcLib.Gui.Waiting
member.summary: This class implement a generic fucntionality for showing a progress dialog box (please wait type) while the main process continues to process some time consuming tasks. The advantage of this class is that, the window is shown in a different thread, making the window user responsive and if the user presses a cancel button a boolen property gets marked that the user cancelled the window. In this case it's the calling programs job to query the Property time to time.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Gui.Waiting.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Default Constructor
member.param: The message to display in the Wait window body
Cancel button should be enabled

member.name: T:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper
member.summary: The SqlHelper class is intended to encapsulate high performance, scalable best practices for common uses of SqlClient.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.AttachParameters(System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter[])
member.summary:
This method is used to attach array of SqlParameters to a SqlCommand.
This method will assign a value of DbNull to any parameter with a direction of InputOutput and a value of null.
This behavior will prevent default values from being used, but this will be the less common case than an intended pure output parameter (derived as InputOutput) where the user provided no input value.
member.param: The command to which the parameters will be added
an array of SqlParameters tho be added to command

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.AssignParameterValues(System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter[],System.Object[])
member.summary: This method assigns an array of values to an array of SqlParameters.
member.param: array of SqlParameters to be assigned values
array of objects holding the values to be assigned

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.PrepareCommand(System.Data.SqlClient.SqlCommand,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlTransaction,System.Data.CommandType,System.String,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter[])
member.summary: This method opens (if necessary) and assigns a connection, transaction, command type and parameters to the provided command.
member.param: the SqlCommand to be prepared
a valid SqlConnection, on which to execute this command
a valid SqlTransaction, or 'null'
the CommandType (stored procedure, text, etc.)
the stored procedure name or T-SQL command
an array of SqlParameters to be associated with the command or 'null' if no parameters are required

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.ExecuteNonQuery(System.String,System.Data.CommandType,System.String)
member.summary: Execute a SqlCommand (that returns no resultset and takes no parameters) against the database specified in the connection string.
member.remarks: e.g.: int result = ExecuteNonQuery(connString, CommandType.StoredProcedure, "PublishOrders");
member.param: a valid connection string for a SqlConnection
the CommandType (stored procedure, text, etc.)
the stored procedure name or T-SQL command
member.returns: an int representing the number of rows affected by the command

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.ExecuteNonQuery(System.String,System.Data.CommandType,System.String,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter[])
member.summary: Execute a SqlCommand (that returns no resultset) against the database specified in the connection string using the provided parameters.
member.remarks: e.g.: int result = ExecuteNonQuery(connString, CommandType.StoredProcedure, "PublishOrders", new SqlParameter("@prodid", 24));
member.param: a valid connection string for a SqlConnection
the CommandType (stored procedure, text, etc.)
the stored procedure name or T-SQL command
an array of SqlParamters used to execute the command
member.returns: an int representing the number of rows affected by the command

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.ExecuteNonQuery(System.String,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Execute a stored procedure via a SqlCommand (that returns no resultset) against the database specified in the connection string using the provided parameter values. This method will query the database to discover the parameters for the stored procedure (the first time each stored procedure is called), and assign the values based on parameter order.
member.param: a valid connection string for a SqlConnection
the name of the stored prcedure
an array of objects to be assigned as the input values of the stored procedure
member.returns: an int representing the number of rows affected by the command

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.ExecuteReader(System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlTransaction,System.Data.CommandType,System.String,System.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter[],AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.SqlConnectionOwnership)
member.summary: Create and prepare a SqlCommand, and call ExecuteReader with the appropriate CommandBehavior.
member.remarks:
If we created and opened the connection, we want the connection to be closed when the DataReader is closed.
If the caller provided the connection, we want to leave it to them to manage.
member.param: a valid SqlConnection, on which to execute this command
a valid SqlTransaction, or 'null'
the CommandType (stored procedure, text, etc.)
the stored procedure name or T-SQL command
an array of SqlParameters to be associated with the command or 'null' if no parameters are required
indicates whether the connection parameter was provided by the caller, or created by SqlHelper
member.returns: SqlDataReader containing the results of the command

the CommandType (stored procedure, text, etc.)
the stored procedure name or T-SQL command
an array of SqlParamters used to execute the command
member.returns: an object containing the value in the 1x1 resultset generated by the command

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.ExecuteScalar(System.Data.SqlClient.SqlTransaction,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Execute a stored procedure via a SqlCommand (that returns a 1x1 resultset) against the specified SqlTransaction using the provided parameter values. This method will query the database to discover the parameters for the stored procedure (the first time each stored procedure is called), and assign the values based on parameter order.
member.param: a valid SqlTransaction
the name of the stored procedure
an array of objects to be assigned as the input values of the stored procedure
member.returns: an object containing the value in the 1x1 resultset generated by the command

member.name: M:AbcLib.Database.SqlHelper.ExecuteXmlReader(System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection,System.Data.CommandType,System.String)
member.summary: Execute a SqlCommand (that returns a resultset and takes no parameters) against the provided SqlConnection.
member.remarks: e.g.: XmlReader r = ExecuteXmlReader(conn, CommandType.StoredProcedure, "GetOrders");
member.param: a valid SqlConnection
the CommandType (stored procedure, text, etc.)
the stored procedure name or T-SQL command using "FOR XML AUTO"
member.returns: an XmlReader containing the resultset generated by the command

member.name: T:AbcLib.Sys.CpuUsageNt
member.summary: Inherits the CPUUsage class and implements the Query method for Windows NT systems.

remarks.p: This class works on Windows NT4, Windows 2000, Windows XP, Windows .NET Server and higher.
You should not use this class directly in your code. Use the CPUUsage.Create() method to instantiate a CPUUsage object.

member.summary: Returns the number of processors in the system in a SYSTEM_BASIC_INFORMATION structure.

member.summary: Returns an opaque SYSTEM_PERFORMANCE_INFORMATION structure.

member.summary: Returns an opaque SYSTEM_TIMEOFDAY_INFORMATION structure.

member.summary: The value returned by NtQuerySystemInformation is no error occurred.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuUsageNt.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new CpuUsageNt instance.
member.exception: One of the system calls fails.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuUsageNt.Query
member.summary: Determines the current average CPU load.
member.returns: An integer that holds the CPU load percentage.
member.exception: One of the system calls fails. The CPU time can not be obtained.

member.name: M:AbcLib.Sys.CpuUsageNt.NtQuerySystemInformation(System.Int32,System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.IntPtr)
member.summary: NtQuerySystemInformation is an internal Windows function that retrieves various kinds of system information.
member.param: One of the values enumerated in SYSTEM_INFORMATION_CLASS, indicating the kind of system information to be retrieved.
Points to a buffer where the requested information is to be returned. The size and structure of this information varies depending on the value of the SystemInformationClass parameter.
Length of the buffer pointed to by the SystemInformation parameter.
Optional pointer to a location where the function writes the actual size of the information requested.
member.returns: Returns a success NTSTATUS if successful, and an NTSTATUS error code otherwise.

member.summary: Holds the old idle time.

member.summary: Holds the old system time.

member.summary: Holds the number of processors in the system.
